True! I was very, very nervous, and I still feel that way. But why do you say I am mad? The disease made my senses sharper and not weaker or dull. The most important thing was hearing very well. It made him feel alert and aware. I heard everything in the sky and on the ground. I heard many sounds in a dark place, and I felt scared. How can I be mad and feel confused about my feelings? Hearken! You can see how calmly I can tell you the whole story. I cannot say how the idea first came to my mind, but it stayed with me. It haunted me day and night because I could not forget it. There was no object there at all. There was no passion in the room and everyone felt bored. I loved the old man because he was kind and friendly to everyone. He never did anything bad to me because he was a good friend. He never insulted me and he treated me well. I did not want his gold because I liked other things more. I think it was his eye, and I feel worried about it. Yes, it was this thing, and I liked it very much. He had the eye of a bird called a vulture. It was a pale blue eye with a film over it. Whenever I felt that way, I became very scared. I slowly decided to take the old man’s life. Then I thought I could stop seeing his eye forever. This is an important moment and it can change things. You think I am crazy and you do not like me. Mad people understand little about the world and life around them. But you really needed to see me. You should have seen how carefully I worked and how thoughtful I was. I took my time, and I planned everything well because I wanted to succeed. I did not rush, so I made good choices along the way. I was very kind to the old man that week before I killed him. Every night, around midnight, I turned the latch on his door. I opened it very gently because I did not want to make noise. Then, I made a hole big enough for my head to fit. I put in a dark lantern that was closed tightly, so no light came out. Then, I pushed my head into the hole. Oh, you would have laughed to see how cleverly I put it in. I moved it
 slowly—very, very slowly, so that I might not disturb the old
 man’s sleep. It took me an hour to place my whole head within the
 opening so far that I could see him as he lay upon his bed. Ha! Would a crazy person be as smart as this? When my head was in the room, I carefully opened the lantern. I did it slowly because the hinges made noise. I opened it just enough so a thin ray of light showed on the vulture's eye. I did this for seven long nights, and I checked at midnight. I always found the old man’s eye closed, so I could not do my work. The old man did not bother me, but his Evil Eye did. Every morning, when the day started, I went into the room. I spoke to him boldly and called him by name in a friendly way. I asked him how he spent the night. He would have been a wise old man to think that I looked at him every night. On the eighth night, I was very careful when I opened the door. A watch’s minute hand moves more quickly than did mine. Never before that night had I felt the extent of my own powers—of
 my sagacity. I could hardly hold back my feelings of success and joy. I was there, opening the door slowly, and he did not know my secret. I laughed at the idea, and maybe he heard me. He moved suddenly on the bed because he seemed surprised. You might think I stepped back, but I did not move at all. His room was very dark because the shutters were closed. I knew he could not see the door. I kept pushing the door steadily to open it. I had my head in, and was about to open the lantern, when my
 thumb slipped upon the tin fastening, and the old man sprang up
 in bed, crying out—“Who’s there? ”

 I kept quite still and said nothing. I did not move at all for one hour. I also did not hear him lie down. He sat up in bed and listened to the sounds. I did this night after night because I heard the death watches in the wall. I heard a soft groan, and I knew it was a sound of fear. It was not a sound of pain or sadness, oh, no! It was a soft sound that comes from deep inside when you feel awe. I knew the sound very well because I heard it many times before. Many nights at midnight, when everyone slept, I felt fear and sadness inside me. The echo of my thoughts became stronger and added to my terrors. I say that I know it well and I remember everything about it. I knew what the old man felt and I felt sorry for him. I chuckled a little inside because of his situation. I knew he had been awake since the first small noise. He turned in bed then. His fears grew stronger, and they have been with him for a long time. He tried to like them for no reason, but he could not. He had been saying to himself—“It is nothing but the wind in
 the chimney—it is only a mouse crossing the floor,” or “It is
 merely a cricket which has made a single chirp. ” Yes, he had been
 trying to comfort himself with these suppositions: but he had
 found all in vain. Death came closer to him and followed him like a dark shadow. It wrapped around him and showed that he was the victim. The sad feeling of the shadow made him aware of my presence in the room. I waited a long time and felt very patient. I did not hear him lie down, so I decided to open a little bit of the lantern. So I opened it carefully and you cannot imagine how slowly. Then, a dim ray of light came out like a thin thread. It fell right on the eye of the vulture. It was open, very wide open, and I felt furious as I looked at it. I saw it with perfect distinctness—all a dull blue, with a
 hideous veil over it that chilled the very marrow in my bones;
 but I could see nothing else of the old man’s face or person: for
 I had directed the ray as if by instinct, precisely upon the
 damned spot. And have I not told you that what you mistake for madness is but
 over-acuteness of the sense? Now, I hear a low and dull sound, like a watch in cotton. I knew that sound very well, and I understood it clearly. It was the beating of the
 old man’s heart. It increased my fury, as the beating of a drum
 stimulates the soldier into courage. But I still did not move and stayed quiet. I hardly breathed because I felt scared and worried about what would happen next. I held the lantern still and looked around carefully in the dark. I tried to see how steady I could keep the light on my eye. Meanwhile, the terrible beating of the heart grew louder and faster. It grew faster and louder with each passing moment. The old man’s terror must have been
 extreme! It grew louder, I say, louder every moment! Do you understand me well and think I am important? I told you I am nervous, so I feel worried now. At midnight, the old house was very quiet and strange noises scared me a lot. I stayed quiet and stood still for a few more minutes. But the noise got louder and louder as time passed. I thought my heart would burst because I felt very scared. A new worry took hold of me because the noise could reach a neighbor. The old man’s hour had come! With a loud yell, I
 threw open the lantern and leaped into the room. He shrieked loudly, and he did it only one time. In a moment, I pulled him to the floor and covered him with the bed. I smiled happily because I saw the work was almost done. But for many minutes, the heart kept beating softly and quietly. This did not bother me, and it could not be heard through the wall. Finally, it stopped making noise and everything became quiet. The old man died, and people felt sad because he was kind. I took away the bed and then looked at the body. Yes, he was dead and cold. I put my hand on my heart and I held it there for many minutes. There was no beating or movement in the quiet place. He was completely dead, and no one could help him anymore. His eye did not bother me anymore. If you still think I am crazy, you will not think so anymore. I will tell you about the smart ways I hid the body. The night got later, and I worked quickly, but I stayed quiet. First, I took apart the body and felt very scared. I cut off the head and the arms. I also cut off the legs. I took three wooden planks from the floor of the room. I then placed all the planks between the thin wooden beams. I then replaced the boards very carefully and no one could see anything wrong. There was nothing to clean and no marks of any kind. There was no blood spot at all. I was very careful because I wanted to be safe. A tub caught everything, and it surprised everyone so much. ha! When I had made an end of these labors, it was four o’clock—still
 dark as midnight. As the bell sounded the hour, there came a
 knocking at the street door. I went down to open it with a happy heart because I had no fear. Three men entered the room and spoke with charm. They said they were police officers. A neighbor heard a loud scream during the night. This made people think of trouble. They told the police about it right away. Then, the police officers came to search the house. I smiled because I did not have anything to fear. I welcomed the gentlemen and made them feel comfortable. I said the loud sound was mine, and I heard it in a dream. The old man I talked about was not here because he was in the country. I showed my visitors around the house and they seemed happy. I asked them to search, and I wanted them to search carefully. I took them to his room after a long time. I showed them his treasures that were safe and undisturbed in the room. Feeling confident, I brought chairs into the room and wanted them here to rest. I placed my own seat in the same spot because the victim lay beneath it. The officers felt happy and satisfied with their work today. My way of acting made them believe me. I felt calm and relaxed in that moment. They sat and talked about familiar things while I answered happily. But soon, I felt my face get pale and I wanted them to leave. My head hurt and I heard a ringing in my ears. They sat and chatted, but I felt unwell. The ringing became louder, and it kept going on. I talked freely to stop the feeling, but it kept getting stronger. Then, I realized the noise was not in my ears. I became very pale, but I spoke more clearly and with a louder voice. The sound became louder, and I did not know what to do. It was a low and dull sound that came quickly. It sounded like a watch in cotton. I gasped for breath, but the officers did not hear me. I talked more quickly and more clearly, but the noise got louder. I got up and argued about small things, but I spoke loudly and moved a lot. The noise grew louder and louder around us. "Why are they still here and not gone yet?" I walked back and forth on the floor with heavy steps. I felt excited and angry because of what I saw. The noise from the men kept getting louder. Oh God! What can I do to help? I was very angry and I shouted a lot. I also used bad words. I moved the chair where I had been sitting, and I scraped it on the floor. The noise was loud, and it kept getting louder. The sound grew louder and louder, and it was hard to ignore. The men talked happily and smiled at each other. Was it possible that they did not hear anything? God is very powerful and he helps people in many ways. —no, no! They heard a loud noise and felt scared because it was dark. They had a feeling that something was wrong, and they suspected it. They knew and understood the truth about what happened that day. They were laughing at me and making fun of my fear. I thought this before, and I still think this now. But anything else was better than this pain and sadness. Anything felt better than this laugh at me and my pain. I could not stand their fake smiles anymore because they made me upset. I felt very scared and I wanted to scream or I might die. "It is happening again and I feel surprised." —hark! louder! louder! louder! _louder! _

 “Villains! ” I shrieked, “dissemble no more! I say that I did it and I accept my responsibility. You should tear up the wooden boards and make space for something new. "Here is a place, and here is another place too!" It is the loud beating of his ugly heart.
